text:
We're planning a larger iteration on "product versions" an concept as old as Otovo itself. I need some help, not with the technical details but with creating a design document to structure and communicate this work to others.

Attached is an empty template that I want you to follow:

```md cloud/product-versions-v2-design-doc.md
# Product Version Deux

Created: November 20, 2024 12:07 PM
Implementation: Not started

This document is scheduled for update and verification by @Fredrik Gyllenhammar  January 1, 2025 

# Introduction

# Scope

## Goals

- What should this feature accomplish

## Non-goals

- What are we not solving in the described feature

## Future Goals

- Explain any natural future goals for the project that was scoped out for any reason now.

# Proposed system

# Release plan

# Dependencies

- Any relevant dependencies should be listed here

# Open questions

- Any open questions that you don’t know the answer to can be listed here for discussion

# Technical details

<aside>
💡 In this section, you should cover any technical details that are relevant to the design. This is only meant for developers and can be as low-level as you want.

</aside>

# Insights

<aside>
💡 Here you should explain how we can extract insights from the data, and any information needed by Product Insights to work with the data generated by the feature. In the future, we would also sketch out and agree on data contracts for the data sent to Insights.

</aside>

# Attachments

<aside>
💡 Add any attachments here that are relevant. For instance source files for any diagrams that are shown in the design document.

</aside>
```


for reference (don't look too hard on it!), heres the existing ProductVersion model


```py cloud/cloud/solarspecs/models.py
from __future__ import annotations

import enum
import uuid
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, TypedDict, cast

from django.contrib.postgres.fields import ArrayField
from django.core.cache import caches
from django.core.validators import MaxValueValidator, MinValueValidator
from django.db import IntegrityError, models
from django.db.models import F, Q
from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _

from choicesenum.django.fields import EnumCharField, EnumIntegerField
from django_deprecate_fields import deprecate_field
from rest_framework.reverse import reverse

from cloud import settings
from cloud.base_models import ImmutableOtovoModel, OtovoModel
from cloud.inventory.enums import (
    BacksheetColor,
    CellType,
    FrameColor,
    GridPhases,
    GridVoltage,
    InverterType,
)
from cloud.location.enums import Country
from cloud.solarspecs.enums import ProductClassification
from cloud.solarspecs.requirements import PanelRequirements

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from django.db.models.fields.related_descriptors import RelatedManager

    from cloud.inventory.models import (
        BatteryModel,
        EvChargerModel,
        InverterModel,
        Manufacturer,
        PanelModel,
        SmartMeterModel,
    )
    from cloud.location.models import Address
    from cloud.mountingsurface.models import MountingSurface
    from cloud.surfaces.models import Surface, Tiling
    from cloud.types import ForeignKey, NullableForeignKey


class ProductVersionModel(OtovoModel):
    country = EnumCharField(
        default=Country.UNKNOWN,
        enum=Country,
        help_text=_('The country this product version belongs to'),
        max_length=2,
    )
    name = models.CharField(
        max_length=254,
        unique=True,
        blank=False,
        help_text=_('Provide an unique descriptive name'),
    )
    panel_minimum_nominal_power = models.IntegerField(
        help_text=_('Minimum nominal power of the panel'),
        blank=False,
    )
    panel_maximum_nominal_power = models.IntegerField(
        help_text=_('Maximum nominal power of the panel'),
        null=True,
        blank=True,
    )
    panel_cell_type = EnumCharField(
        enum=CellType,
        default=CellType.UNKNOWN,
        help_text=_('The cell type of the panel'),
        max_length=20,
        blank=True,
    )
    backsheet_color = EnumCharField(
        enum=BacksheetColor,
        default=BacksheetColor.UNKNOWN,
        help_text=_('The backsheet color of the panel'),
        max_length=20,
    )
    frame_color = EnumCharField(
        enum=FrameColor,
        default=FrameColor.UNKNOWN,
        help_text=_('The frame color of the panel'),
        max_length=20,
    )
    minimum_panel_product_warranty = models.IntegerField(
        help_text=_('Minimum product warranty of the panel in years'),
        blank=True,
        null=True,
    )
    maximum_panel_product_warranty = models.IntegerField(
        help_text=_('Maximum product warranty of the panel in years'),
        blank=True,
        null=True,
    )
    product_classification = EnumCharField(
        enum=ProductClassification,
        default=ProductClassification.UNKNOWN,
        help_text=_('Product classification of the panel'),
        max_length=20,
    )
    enabled = models.BooleanField(
        default=False,
        help_text=_('Is the product version enabled for sales in its associated country'),
    )

    enabled_for_live_products = deprecate_field(
        ArrayField(
            EnumCharField(enum=Country, max_length=2),
            blank=True,
            default=list,
            verbose_name=_('Enable this product version for calculations'),
            help_text=_('Whether it can be used for the calculations'),
        )
    )
    enabled_for_projects = deprecate_field(
        ArrayField(
            EnumCharField(enum=Country, max_length=2),
            blank=True,
            default=list,
            verbose_name=_('Enable this product version for projects'),
            help_text=_('Whether it can be used by the Project Managers'),
        )
    )
    enabled_for_costmodels = deprecate_field(
        ArrayField(
            EnumCharField(enum=Country, max_length=2),
            blank=True,
            default=list,
            verbose_name=_('Enable this product version for the costmodels'),
            help_text=_('Whether it can be used for the panels in the costmodels'),
        )
    )

    class Meta(OtovoModel.Meta):
        ordering = ('-created_at',)
        permissions = [
            (
                'enable_product_version_for_primary_bu_country',
                'Can enable and disable product version for country of primary business unit',
            ),
            (
                'enable_product_version_all_countries',
                'Can enable and disable product version for all countries',
            ),
        ]

    def __str__(self):
        return f'{self.name}'

    @property
    def panel_requirements(self) -> PanelRequirements:
        return PanelRequirements(
            panel_model_id=None,
            panel_maximum_nominal_power=self.panel_maximum_nominal_power,
            panel_minimum_nominal_power=self.panel_minimum_nominal_power,
            panel_cell_type=self.panel_cell_type,
            backsheet_color=self.backsheet_color,
            frame_color=self.frame_color,
            minimum_panel_product_warranty=self.minimum_panel_product_warranty,
            maximum_panel_product_warranty=self.maximum_panel_product_warranty,
        )

    @staticmethod
    def get_by_name(name: str):
        if product_version := caches['localmemory'].get(name):
            return product_version

        product_version = ProductVersionModel.objects.get(name=name)
        caches['localmemory'].set(name, product_version)
        return product_version

    def get_absolute_url(self):
        return '{}{}'.format(
            settings.CLOUD_URL,
            reverse(
                'inventory:product-version-detail',
                kwargs={'pk': self.pk},
                urlconf='cloud.urls',
            ),
        )

    @classmethod
    def get_products_with_classification(cls, classification: ProductClassification):
        return cls.objects.filter(product_classification=classification)

    @property
    def display_name(self):
        classification = self.product_classification
        colors = ''
        if self.backsheet_color != BacksheetColor.UNKNOWN and self.frame_color != FrameColor.UNKNOWN:
            colors = f', {self.backsheet_color.display}/{self.frame_color.display}'
        return f'{classification.display} {self.panel_minimum_nominal_power}W{colors}'

    @classmethod
    def _get_live_products(cls, country: Country) -> dict[ProductClassification, ProductVersionModel]:
        return {
            version.product_classification: version
            for version in (
                ProductVersionModel.objects.filter(country=country, enabled=True)
                .order_by('product_classification', '-created_at')
                .distinct('product_classification')
            )
        }

    @classmethod
    def get_live_products(cls, country: Country) -> dict[ProductClassification, ProductVersionModel]:
        value = caches['default'].get_or_set(
            f'product-version-model-live-products-{country}',
            lambda: cls._get_live_products(country),
            60,
        )
        return cast('dict[ProductClassification, ProductVersionModel]', value)


class LegacyProductVerionNamedPanelType(TypedDict):
    panel_requirements: PanelRequirements
    product_classification: ProductClassification
    name: str
    enabled: bool


class LegacyProductVerionPanelType(TypedDict):
    panel_requirements: PanelRequirements
    product_classification: ProductClassification
    enabled: bool

    # TODO remove during planning cleanup
    @classmethod
    def get_products_with_classification(cls, classification: ProductClassification):
        return [p for p in cls if p.value['product_classification'] == classification]

    @classmethod
    def get_enabled_versions(cls):
        return [p for p in cls if p.value['enabled']]

    @classmethod
    def get_by_name(cls, name: str):
        try:
            return cls[name]
        except KeyError as ex:
            raise ValueError(f'Invalid product classification {name}') from ex

    def display_name(self):
        requirements = self.value['panel_requirements']
        classification = self.value['product_classification']
        colors = ''
        if (
            requirements.backsheet_color != BacksheetColor.UNKNOWN
            and requirements.frame_color != FrameColor.UNKNOWN
        ):
            colors = f', {requirements.backsheet_color.display}/{requirements.frame_color.display}'
        return f'{classification.display} {requirements.panel_minimum_nominal_power}W{colors}'


class Spec(ImmutableOtovoModel):  # type: ignore
    """
    Store information that forms the basis for one request to the cost and price engine.

    Each spec consists of multiple related objects
    ----------------------------------------------
    From a business logic perspective, the term “spec” describes the entirety of one Spec object and all
    SpecBuilding and SpecSurface objects that belong to it.

    Requirements restrict the selection of hardware
    -----------------------------------------------
    In the spec context, the term “requirement” describes an optional filtering parameter that can restrict
    the hardware selection if needed. For example, specifying value in SpecBuilding.inverter_manufacturer will
    force the cost and price engine to select an inverter of that manufacturer. If that field is empty, on the
    other hand, the engine will choose whatever it prefers based on its business logic. Requirement fields
    currently exist on SpecBuilding and SpecSurface. Model-level constants (e. g. INVERTER_REQUIREMENT_FIELDS)
    declare which subset of the model’s fields represent requirements, thus enabling for example to iterate
    over all of them.
    """

    if TYPE_CHECKING:
        spec_buildings: RelatedManager[SpecBuilding]
        address: ForeignKey[Address]

    hardware_selection_override = deprecate_field(
        models.OneToOneField(
            'costengine.HardwareSelection',
            on_delete=models.PROTECT,
            null=True,
            related_name='+',
            verbose_name='Hardware selection override',
            help_text=(
                'If this hardware selection is given, the cost and price engine will merely calculate a cost and '
                'a price for the specified hardware.'
            ),
        )
    )

    manual_mode = models.BooleanField(
        default=False,
        null=True,
        verbose_name='Enable manual mode',
        help_text=(
            'Cost engine will not care whether provided hardware is compatible and just '
            'calculate the cost/price if this field is set to True.'
        ),
    )

    address = models.ForeignKey('location.Address', on_delete=models.PROTECT, verbose_name='Address')
    snow_load = models.DecimalField(
        max_digits=3,
        decimal_places=1,
        verbose_name='Snow load (kN/m2)',
        help_text=(
            'The maximum force per square metre we should expect for snow to exert on a roof surface. '
            'Depends on the geographical location.'
        ),
    )
    is_farm_or_business = models.BooleanField(verbose_name='Is farm or business')

    grid_voltage = EnumIntegerField(
        enum=GridVoltage,
        verbose_name='Grid voltage',
        help_text='A technical property of the grid that is required to select the correct inverter.',
    )
    grid_phases = EnumIntegerField(
        enum=GridPhases,
        verbose_name='Number of grid phases',
        help_text=(
            'A technical property of the grid that is required to select the correct inverter. With three '
            'phases you can deliver more power in a cable than with one phase. The inverter must match the '
            'number of phases in the grid.'
        ),
    )
    multiple_one_phase_inverters_allowed = models.BooleanField(
        verbose_name='Multiple one-phase inverters allowed',
        help_text=(
            'This allows to select more than one single phase inverter. Normally Cloud tries to find exactly '
            'one inverter that fits.'
        ),
    )
    max_single_phase_inverter_in_single_phase_grid = models.PositiveIntegerField(
        verbose_name=(
            'Maximum total nominal power of single phase inverter(s) in a single phase grid (watts).'
        ),
        help_text=(
            'This is a market-specific setting that reflects legal restrictions. If the system power is too '
            'high, it could disturb the grid.'
        ),
    )
    max_single_phase_inverter_in_three_phase_grid = models.PositiveIntegerField(
        verbose_name='Maximum total nominal power of single phase inverter(s) in a three phase grid (watts)',
        help_text=(
            'This is a market-specific setting that reflects legal restrictions. If the system power is too '
            'high, it could disturb the grid.'
        ),
    )

    product_version = models.ForeignKey(
        ProductVersionModel, on_delete=models.PROTECT, null=True, verbose_name='Product version'
    )


class SpecBuilding(ImmutableOtovoModel):
    """
    Store spec information about a single building.
    """

    if TYPE_CHECKING:
        spec_surfaces: RelatedManager[SpecSurface]
        battery_manufacturer: NullableForeignKey[Manufacturer]
        battery_model: NullableForeignKey[BatteryModel]
        battery_compatible_inverter_model: NullableForeignKey[InverterModel]
        ev_charger_model: NullableForeignKey[EvChargerModel]
        inverter_manufacturer: NullableForeignKey[Manufacturer]
        inverter_model: NullableForeignKey[InverterModel]
        smart_meter_manufacturer: NullableForeignKey[Manufacturer]
        smart_meter_model: NullableForeignKey[SmartMeterModel]

    spec = models.ForeignKey(
        Spec, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='spec_buildings', verbose_name='Spec'
    )

    built_on = models.DateField(null=True, verbose_name='Date when the building was built')

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # SYSTEM POWER
    system_power = models.PositiveIntegerField(
        verbose_name='System power (watts)',
        help_text='The nominal power generated by all panels on this building together.',
    )

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # BUILDING EQUIPMENT
    num_dc_strings = models.PositiveSmallIntegerField(
        verbose_name='Number of DC strings',
        help_text=(
            'A DC string connects multiple panels in a series. Within one string, the voltage of the panels '
            'adds up, and there is a maximum voltage that one string can carry.'
        ),
    )
    num_dc_switches = models.PositiveSmallIntegerField(
        verbose_name='Number of DC switches',
        help_text=(
            'A DC switch is a physical switch that allows to cut off the current coming from one DC string. '
            'In the case of an emergency such as fire, it allows to isolate these panels from the rest of '
            'the system.'
        ),
    )
    dc_string_extra_meters = models.PositiveSmallIntegerField(
        verbose_name='DC string extra meters',
        help_text=(
            'If the required length exceeds a standard length defined per market. DC strings conduct current '
            'from panels to the inverter.'
        ),
    )
    ac_cable_extra_meters = models.PositiveSmallIntegerField(
        verbose_name='AC cable extra meters',
        help_text=(
            'If the required length exceeds a standard length defined per market. AC strings conduct current '
            'from the inverter and onwards.'
        ),
    )

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # BATTERY SPEC
    battery_required = models.BooleanField(default=False, verbose_name='Battery required')
    # Optional requirements
    # NOTE: To make sure that we limit the selection only when desired, all optional requirement fields
    #       1.  MUST be nullable, and
    #       2.  MUST NOT define a default value.
    BATTERY_REQUIREMENT_FIELDS = [
        'battery_manufacturer',
        'battery_model',
        'battery_has_backup',
        'battery_compatible_inverter_model',
        'battery_is_ac',
        'battery_minimum_capacity',
    ]
    battery_manufacturer = models.ForeignKey(
        'inventory.Manufacturer',
        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
        null=True,
        related_name='+',
        verbose_name='Battery manufacturer',
    )
    battery_model = models.ForeignKey(
        'inventory.BatteryModel', on_delete=models.PROTECT, null=True, verbose_name='Battery model'
    )
    battery_has_backup = models.BooleanField(
        null=True,
        verbose_name='Battery has backup',
        help_text=(
            'This capability allows the user to consume power form their own battery if the power grid in '
            'their area goes down.'
        ),
    )
    battery_compatible_inverter_model = models.ForeignKey(
        to='inventory.InverterModel',
        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
        verbose_name='Battery-compatible inverter model',
        help_text=(
            'If set, the battery must be compatible with this inverter model. We set this when adding a '
            'battery to an existing installation where this inverter model is already installed.'
        ),
        null=True,
        related_name='+',
    )
    battery_is_ac = models.BooleanField(
        null=True,
        verbose_name='Battery is AC',
    )
    battery_minimum_capacity = models.PositiveIntegerField(
        null=True,
        verbose_name='Battery minimum capacity (Wh)',
        help_text='If set, the battery must have at least this capacity.',
    )

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # EV CHARGER SPEC
    ev_charger_required = models.BooleanField(default=False, verbose_name='EV charger required')
    # Optional requirements
    # NOTE: To make sure that we limit the selection only when desired, all optional requirement fields
    #       1.  MUST be nullable, and
    #       2.  MUST NOT define a default value.
    EV_CHARGER_REQUIREMENT_FIELDS = ['ev_charger_model']
    ev_charger_model = models.ForeignKey(
        'inventory.EvChargerModel', on_delete=models.PROTECT, null=True, verbose_name='EV charger model'
    )

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # INVERTER SPEC
    inverter_required = models.BooleanField(default=False, verbose_name='Inverter required')
    # Optional requirements
    # NOTE: To make sure that we limit the selection only when desired, all optional requirement fields
    #       1.  MUST be nullable, and
    #       2.  MUST NOT define a default value.
    INVERTER_REQUIREMENT_FIELDS = [
        'inverter_manufacturer',
        'inverter_model',
        'inverter_is_hybrid',
        'inverter_is_battery_inverter',
        'inverter_type',
    ]
    inverter_manufacturer = models.ForeignKey(
        'inventory.Manufacturer',
        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
        null=True,
        related_name='+',
        verbose_name='Inverter manufacturer',
    )
    inverter_model = models.ForeignKey(
        'inventory.InverterModel', on_delete=models.PROTECT, null=True, verbose_name='Inverter model'
    )
    inverter_is_hybrid = models.BooleanField(
        null=True,
        verbose_name='Inverter is hybrid',
        help_text='A hybrid inverter can connect to both solar panels and a battery.',
    )
    inverter_is_battery_inverter = models.BooleanField(
        null=True,
        verbose_name='Inverter is battery inverter',
        help_text='A battery inverter can only connect to a battery.',
    )
    inverter_type = EnumCharField(
        null=True,
        max_length=100,
        enum=InverterType,
        verbose_name='Inverter type',
    )
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # SMART METER SPEC
    smart_meter_required = models.BooleanField(default=False, verbose_name='Smart meter required')
    # Optional requirements
    # NOTE: To make sure that we limit the selection only when desired, all optional requirement fields
    #       1.  MUST be nullable, and
    #       2.  MUST NOT define a default value.
    SMART_METER_REQUIREMENT_FIELDS = [
        'smart_meter_manufacturer',
        'smart_meter_model',
    ]
    smart_meter_manufacturer = models.ForeignKey(
        'inventory.Manufacturer',
        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
        null=True,
        related_name='+',
        verbose_name='Smart meter manufacturer',
    )
    smart_meter_model = models.ForeignKey(
        'inventory.SmartMeterModel', on_delete=models.PROTECT, null=True, verbose_name='Smart meter model'
    )
    smart_meter_is_hems = models.BooleanField(
        null=True,
        verbose_name='Smart meter is HEMS',
        help_text='Whether the smart meter is a Home Energy Management System.',
    )

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # OTHER

    # Note: This field is not used for pricing
    #  Historically, building data was stored in a JSON-field, in PriceSpec.cost_response.
    #  This ID is the ID that was given to the building in that JSON-field. When migrating
    #  data from the cost response into Django models, we decided to store this to help ease
    #  our migration effort. Without it, we're not able to correlate buildings in the old
    #  JSON structure to `SpecBuilding`s in the database.
    #  Once we drop cost_response from PriceSpec, this can also be dropped.
    cost_response_building_id = models.CharField(max_length=36)

    class Meta:
        constraints = [
            models.CheckConstraint(
                name='battery_required_must_be_true_if_any_battery_requirement_is_given',
                violation_error_message=(
                    'If one or more battery requirements are set, battery_required must be True'
                ),
                check=~(
                    Q(battery_required=False)
                    & (
                        Q(battery_manufacturer__isnull=False)
                        | Q(battery_model__isnull=False)
                        | Q(battery_has_backup__isnull=False)
                        | Q(battery_compatible_inverter_model__isnull=False)
                    )
                ),
            ),
            models.CheckConstraint(
                name='ev_charger_required_must_be_true_if_any_ev_charger_requirement_is_given',
                violation_error_message=(
                    'If one or more EV charger requirements are set, ev_charger_required must be True'
                ),
                check=~(Q(ev_charger_required=False) & Q(ev_charger_model__isnull=False)),
            ),
            models.CheckConstraint(
                name='inverter_required_must_be_true_if_any_inverter_requirement_is_given',
                violation_error_message=(
                    'If one or more inverter requirements are set, inverter_required must be True'
                ),
                check=~(
                    Q(inverter_required=False)
                    & (
                        Q(inverter_manufacturer__isnull=False)
                        | Q(inverter_model__isnull=False)
                        | Q(inverter_is_hybrid__isnull=False)
                        | Q(inverter_is_battery_inverter__isnull=False)
                    )
                ),
            ),
            models.CheckConstraint(
                name='smart_meter_required_must_be_true_if_any_smart_meter_requirement_is_given',
                violation_error_message=(
                    'If one or more smart meter requirements are set, smart_meter_required must be True'
                ),
                check=~(
                    Q(smart_meter_required=False)
                    & (Q(smart_meter_manufacturer__isnull=False) | Q(smart_meter_model__isnull=False))
                ),
            ),
        ]


class SpecSurface(ImmutableOtovoModel):
    """
    Store spec information about a single roof surface of a building.
    """

    if TYPE_CHECKING:
        sales_surface: ForeignKey[Surface]
        tiling: NullableForeignKey[Tiling]
        mounting_surface: ForeignKey[MountingSurface]
        panel_model: NullableForeignKey[PanelModel]

    spec_building = models.ForeignKey(
        SpecBuilding, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='spec_surfaces', verbose_name='Spec building'
    )
    sales_surface = models.ForeignKey(
        'surfaces.Surface',
        related_name='spec_surfaces',
        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
        verbose_name='Sales-related surface',
    )
    # NOTE: A sales surface can have multiple tilings, which then represent different planning variations that
    #       sales agents create in the Cloud interface. Therefore, the spec-side surface needs to know which
    #       tiling it was created for.
    tiling = models.ForeignKey(
        'tetris.Tiling',
        related_name='spec_surfaces',
        on_delete=models.PROTECT,
        verbose_name='Tiling',
        null=True,
    )

    eave_height = models.DecimalField(
        max_digits=5,
        decimal_places=2,
        validators=[MinValueValidator(limit_value=0)],
        verbose_name='Eave height',
        help_text=(
            'The minimum height in meters from the ground to the surface’s eave. The eave is the lowest part '
            'of the roof.'
        ),
    )
    slope = models.IntegerField(
        help_text='Integer in range 0 to 90 inclusive. 0 is a horizontal surface, 90 is a vertical surface.',
        verbose_name='Slope',
        validators=[MinValueValidator(limit_value=0), MaxValueValidator(limit_value=90)],
    )
    mounting_surface = models.ForeignKey(
        'mountingsurface.MountingSurface', on_delete=models.PROTECT, verbose_name='Mounting surface'
    )

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # PANEL SPEC
    num_panels_total = models.PositiveSmallIntegerField(verbose_name='Total number of panels')
    num_panels_horizontal = models.PositiveSmallIntegerField(
        default=0,
        verbose_name='Number of horizontal panels',
        help_text=(
            'The standard mounting orientation is vertical. Horizontal mounting requires other equipment and '
            'is usually more expensive.'
        ),
    )
    num_optimizers = models.PositiveSmallIntegerField(default=0, verbose_name='Number of optimizers')
    num_dc_strings = models.PositiveSmallIntegerField(default=1, verbose_name='Number of DC strings')
    # Optional requirements
    # NOTE: To make sure that we limit the selection only when desired, all optional requirement fields
    #       1.  MUST be nullable, and
    #       2.  MUST NOT define a default value.
    panel_model = models.ForeignKey(
        'inventory.PanelModel', on_delete=models.PROTECT, null=True, verbose_name='Panel model'
    )

    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
        if not self.spec_building.spec.product_version:
            raise IntegrityError('product_version on Spec cannot be null when a surface exists.')
        super().save(*args, **kwargs)

    class Meta:
        constraints = [
            models.CheckConstraint(
                name='num_panels_horizontal_lte_num_panels_total',
                violation_error_message='num_panels_horizontal must be smaller than or equal to num_panels_total',
                check=Q(num_panels_horizontal__lte=F('num_panels_total')),
            ),
        ]
```



Here's a general outline of what we're thinking for v2 (not structured as the design doc, so please just use this as context)

```md
## The Spec

The "Spec" defines a set of (mostly) optional questions  that a valid cost must be able to answer.

- Panels required?
  - How many?
  - Min power?
  - Max Power?
  - Color?
  - Warenty?
- Battery required?
  - What manufacturer?
    - What model?
  - Minimum capacity?
  - AC?
- Inverters required?
  - What manufacturer?
    - What model?
  - Minimum capacity?
- (...)


## The Product Version
The "Product Version" defines a set of answers to some of these questions. The idea of V2 is to answer all of the questions:

### V1
- Panels required?
  - How many?
  - Min power?
  - Max Power?
  - Color?
  - Warenty?
  


### V2
- Panels required?
  - What manufacturer?
    - What model?
  - How many?
  - Min power?
  - Max Power?
  - Color?
  - Warenty?
- Battery required?
  - What manufacturer?
    - What model?
  - Minimum capacity?
  - AC?
- Inverters required?
  - What manufacturer?
    - What model?
  - Minimum capacity?
- (...)


## Sales funnel

We imagine adding support for defining a product version both in product pages and the iframe by adding a queryparam, so that specific versions are pre-selected when customer create leads.

We need to change the sales funnel to support this. Today its oriented around "choose panels between standard, premium and performance" - and these are hard coded. In v2 things can be oriented around penels anymore - we need to change this to be more generic, probably having the "version" or "package" as a top-level entity in the information hierarchy for customers.

## Campaigns

We're thinking of adding support for restricting a campaign to a product verision - so we can run specific haredware campaigns "This month you get 10% off on Huawei inverters" or "Get a free upgrade to the new LG panels" etc.


## Together these minor changes whould allow for 

- Hardware specific customer jouneys
- Hardware campaigns
- Brand partnerships
- Actual tiers of packages in all hardware categories
- etc


## Offers

This would require a change in how the offer creation page deals with classifications. The Product Version today, forces a set of requirements onto the spec. These requirements are only on the panel, but they cannot be overridden. If we add more requirements to the product version, we add more restrictions onto the sales agent

Note: Offers do not need a product version, nothing about the offer page is oriented around the chosen classification.

### Solution 1
Add the “missing” panel requirements and change the interaction such that when a classification is selected, the requirements are pre-filled onto the form, they are then used to make a selection, but the agent is also free to modify the requirements.

### Solution 2
Allow agents to select “No classification”. This means no product version is used at all. Panels are required if there is a surface, but that’s it. The agent chooses everything themselves


## Opportunities
While we’re “in the area” - here are adjacent things we might consider:

- “Debug tool” for sales agents. This is a recurring problem today (“Why can’t I find any prices?”), where sales agents some times end up in a situation where the system can't give them any prices. There's a risk that with the new system, things are more _flexible_, leading to a higher chance of creating incompatible configurations that can't be priced. A debug tool could help them understand why they can't get a price, and what they need to do to get one.


```

# Product Version Deux

Created: November 20, 2024 12:07 PM
Implementation: Not started

This document is scheduled for update and verification by @Fredrik Gyllenhammar January 1, 2025 

# Introduction

Product Versions in Otovo have historically been used to define panel requirements and classifications (Standard, Premium, Performance) for different markets. This system has served us well but is limited in scope. Product Version V2 aims to expand this concept to cover all hardware components and enable new business opportunities through hardware-specific customer journeys and campaigns.

# Scope

## Goals

- Extend Product Versions to cover all hardware components (panels, batteries, inverters, etc.)
- Enable hardware-specific customer journeys through product pages and iframe integration
- Support hardware-specific campaigns and brand partnerships
- Provide more flexibility in package/tier definitions beyond panel classifications
- Maintain backward compatibility with existing panel-based product versions
- Improve transparency and debugging capabilities for sales agents

## Non-goals

- Changes to the cost engine's core functionality
- Modifications to hardware compatibility rules
- Changes to the pricing logic
- Redesign of the entire sales funnel UI/UX (only adapting existing flows)

## Future Goals

- Advanced campaign management system for hardware-specific promotions
- Sophisticated A/B testing capabilities for different product versions
- Integration with manufacturer-specific inventory management
- Automated product version optimization based on market performance

# Proposed system

The new system will:

1. Expand Product Version Model to include:
   - Full hardware specification capabilities
   - Campaign-related fields
   - Market-specific configurations

2. Modify the sales funnel to:
   - Support product version selection via query parameters
   - Present hardware packages as primary choices instead of panel classifications
   - Handle version-specific requirements throughout the process

3. Implement two possible approaches for offer creation:
   - Solution 1: Pre-filled but modifiable requirements
   - Solution 2: Optional classification with full agent control

1. Phase 1: Model changes and database migrations
2. Phase 2: Sales funnel adaptation
3. Phase 3: Campaign integration
4. Phase 4: Offer creation changes
5. Phase 5: Debug tools implementation

- Sales funnel frontend components

- How do we ensure performance with potentially complex hardware requirement combinations?
- Should we maintain the concept of classifications in V2?
